observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Determine existence of records to support the recorded balance of the Due to NGAs account, and in case of absence of documents, prepare its aging schedule and recommend to the Municipal Mayor to file a request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated fund transfers to the COA ATL and/or SA to clean-up said account for its fair presentation in the financial statements; and
b. Immediately prepare the necessary JEV to record the liquidation of fund transfers totaling ₱1.499 million.

observations.reason: The balance was reduced to ₱2.476 million as of December 31, 2024. On-going implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Manapla Municipal Government Employees Union (MMGEU) to return the amount transferred by the municipality to the union since the financial assistance violates Section 305(b) of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.
observations.reason: Demand Letter was already sent to the Union. The balance was reduced to ₱2.394 million as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary journal entries to derecognize the donated Level II Water System
Project in Barangay San Pablo to present accurate and reliable balances in
the PPE and Equity accounts in the financial statements as at year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to coordinate with the Municipal
Accounting Office regarding the status of the CIP projects and determine
whether there are completed projects from prior years that were not
transferred to the appropriate PPE accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the PPE
and CIP accounts based on the Certificates of Project Completion and
Acceptance submitted by the MEO and compute the related depreciation
of the asset account.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understated Other
Payables due to erroneous entry on interest on loans for fair presentation
in the financial statements, as follows:
Prior Period Adjustment ₱1,783,023.79
Other Payables ₱1,783,023.79
b. Reconcile the forwarded balances at ₱476,295.94 and prepare the
necessary journal entries to adjust the negative balances to present
accurate and reliable balances of Other Payables and Other affected
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor
expenditures incurred at year-end and strictly adhere to the accrual basis of
accounting by preparing the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to recognize the
expenses and payables.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately
reconcile their respective records, for preparation of adjustments
accordingly and to conduct regular reconciliations on the Cash in Bank
– LCCA balances; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer to comply strictly with Section 27 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs (Vol. II) by ensuring that a cashbook is maintained for
each bank account and by fund and is updated and balanced daily.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require all requisitioning offices to timely prepare and submit the SSMI
to the MAO for the preparation of JEVs in recognizing expenses for the
supplies already consumed or distributed;
b. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the required periodic
physical inventory count and prepare and submit the RPCI not later than
July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester,
respectively, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records of
Inventories with the RPCI.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Cancel the checks and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II in case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed;
b. If there is no request, immediately cancel the stale checks and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
c. Coordinate and monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the stale check at least one month before the check becomes stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Ensure that all the PPAs funded by the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 to attain the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality;
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer/Project In-charge to expedite the implementation of PPAs so that the desired benefits and services from these projects are delivered to the target beneficiaries without delay; and
c. Direct the MPDC to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of all PPAs funded by the 20% DF so that the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the necessary disbursement voucher and check, respectively for the reversion of ₱4.869 million unutilized LDRRMF for the years prior to 2015 up to 2017 under the STF to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund so that said amount could be utilized for other social services projects and activities after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan, pursuant to the last paragraph of Section 21 RA No. 10121 and Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the MDRRM Officer and all other implementing offices to implement all projects under the LDRRMF based on the target timelines that should be indicated in the LDRRMFIP to ensure optimum utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay and to equip the Municipality against the adverse impacts of disaster or calamity that may occur anytime.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that:
a.) In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
b.) If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
c.) Coordinate and monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks at least one month before the checks become stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

It is further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or designated GSO and the Municipal Officer exert effort to locate all land titles of land owned by the Municipality and process the titling of land acquired through donation, or other modes of acquisition Land Registration Authority, where necessary.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a.) Inventory Committee to prepare the PIP and complete the conduct of the physical count of PPE by determining the properties found at station, missing or non-existing PPEs and the condition of the property items whether serviceable or not and submit the RPCPPE for all properties owned by the Municipality;
b.) MAO and the Property Unit to undertake and complete the procedures under Items 6.3.1 and 6.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
c.) MAO and the Property Unit to undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the PCs maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.
observations.reason: Physical count was not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a.) Instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Officer and all other implementing offices to implement all projects funded by the LDRRMF based on the target timelines indicated in the MDRRM Plan to ensure optimal utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay and to equip the municipality against the adverse impacts of disaster or calamity;
b.) Instruct the MDRRM Council to prepare a revised MDRRMF Annual Investment Plan to serve as basis in the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF transferred to the STF and ensure that the programs, projects and activities indicated in said plan must be implemented based on timelines; and
c.) Direct the MDRRM Officer to properly utilize the LDRRMF in adherence to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We reiterated the following recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant:
a. Enforce immediate settlement of long outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; 
b. Impose the withholding of salary and the institution of other sanctions to all employees who could not settle their cash advances after demand letters were issued to them as provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
c. Closely coordinate with COA-ICFAU regarding the status of previously submitted liquidation documents or charges against previous years’ confidential fund so that said office could correspondingly issue a credit advice or credit notice.
observations.reason: The balance was reduced to ₱2.853 million as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Manapla Municipal Government Employees Union (MMGEU) to return the amount transferred by the municipality to the Union since the financial assistance violates Section 305(b) of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.
observations.reason: Demand Letter was already sent to the Union since the financial assistance violates Section 305(b) of RA 7160. The balance was reduced to ₱2.494 million as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
3.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Cancel the checks and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II in case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed;

b. If there is no request, immediately cancel the stale checks and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and

c. Coordinate and monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the stale check at least one month before the check becomes stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

4.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. Determine existence of records to support the recorded balance of the Due to NGAs account, and in case of absence of documents, prepare its aging schedule and recommend to the Municipal Mayor to file a request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated fund transfers to the COA ATL and/or SA to clean-up said account for its fair presentation in the financial statements; and

b. Immediately prepare the necessary JEV to record the liquidation of fund transfers totaling ₱1.499 million.

5.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

a. Ensure that all the PPAs funded by the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 to attain the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality;

b. Direct the Municipal Engineer/Project In-charge to expedite the implementation of PPAs so that the desired benefits and services from these projects are delivered to the target beneficiaries without delay; and

c. Direct the MPDC to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of all PPAs funded by the 20% DF so that the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

6.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the necessary disbursement voucher and check, respectively for the reversion of ₱4.869 million unutilized LDRRMF for the years prior to 2015 up to 2017 under the STF to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund so that said amount could be utilized for other social services projects and activities after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan, pursuant to the last paragraph of Section 21 RA No. 10121 and Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

7.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the MDRRM Officer and all other implementing offices to implement all projects funded out of the LDRRMF based on the target timelines that should be indicated in the LDRRMFIP to ensure optimum utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay and to equip the Municipality against the adverse impacts of disaster or calamity that may occur anytime.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a.) Municipal Health Officer to submit to the MAO all the necessary documents pertaining to the vaccines received and record the receipt of vaccines as grants and donations in kind; and
b.) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the remaining unutilized vaccines inventories as of year-end, in compliance with Paragraphs 96 and 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that the BRS for all funds are prepared on time and the corresponding JEVs are drawn to take up all valid book reconciling items to ascertain the correctness of the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that:
a.) In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
b.) If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
c.) Coordinate and monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks at least one month before the checks become stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare JEV for the reversion of the ₱2.277 million unutilized balance for the years 2015 and 2016 under the STF to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund so that the amount could be utilized for other social services projects after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan pursuant to Section 5.1.13 of JC No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Management agreed to facilitate the issuance of the appropriate Executive Order to establish the Municipality’s GAD M&E Team to formalize the monitoring of GAD related activities and ensure its pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended and the Management agreed to submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Reports within the deadline set in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to expedite the preparation of Program of Works, Plans and Designs and other documents for all unimplemented projects under the 20% DF so that the desired benefits and services from these projects are delivered to the target beneficiaries without delay; and
b. Ensure that all planned projects under the 20% DF are optimally utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Officer and Management to implement all projects funded by the LDRRMF based on the target timelines indicated in the MDRRM Plan to ensure optimum utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay and to equip the municipality against the adverse impacts of disaster or calamity.

It is further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or designated GSO and the Municipal Officer exert effort to locate all land titles of land owned by the Municipality and process the titling of land acquired through donation, or other modes of acquisition Land Registration Authority, where necessary.
observations.reason: Inventory of land and titling are partially completed.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a.	Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct annually a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s PPE as of year-end and submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year pursuant to Section 124, MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

b.	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards for PPE in compliance with Section 119 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and

c.	Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the required reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards and property cards on PPE pursuant to Section 114, MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF is discussed in the Notes to the Financial Statements in compliance with the requirements of Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a special task force to closely coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office that will work on titling of all real properties purchased, donated or acquired by the city government which has remained untitled.

It is further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or designated GSO and the Municipal Officer exert effort to locate all land titles of land owned by the Municipality and process the titling of land acquired through donation, or other modes of acquisition Land Registration Authority, where necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Create an Inventory Committee in consonance with the composition provided under Item 5.2 and require them to complete all the preliminary activities and prepare the Physical Inventory Plan before the conduct of inventory taking;
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s PPE whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station and undertake all planned activities/procedures in the approved PIP and prepare the RPCPPE pursuant to Item 6.2.10 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to update its property records based on the result of the physical inventory and reconcile these with the accounting records to come up with the reliable balances of PPE accounts pursuant to Item 5.12 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Agriculturist conduct inventory of all breeding stocks, submit complete inventory report thereof and provide the prevailing market price for each as basis of the City Accountant in maintaining an accurate and reliable Breeding Stocks account in the books, pursuant to Items 4.5.1 and 4.5.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and IPSAS 27.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discharge his duties with utmost diligence by posting collections and deposits in the cash book daily and reconcile his cash hand with the cash book balance also daily as well as reconcile the balance with the accounting records at least quarterly pursuant to Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I and Section 111 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and/or the Municipal Treasurer:
In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks especially those checks which have been outstanding for over two years and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
Hereafter, the two offices coordinate and monitor checks already issued but remain outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the stale check at least one month before the check becomes stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up all valid book reconciling items at once after the bank reconciliation process is finished pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ascertain the correctness of balances of Cash-in-Bank-LCCA.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer monitor the expiration dates of the COVID-19 vaccines donated to the Municipality and prioritize the utilization chronologically from the earliest to the latest. We also recommended that the MHO coordinate with the Provincial Health Office as to the proper disposal of the expired vaccines.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify as non-current liability the Loans Payable – Domestic account amounting to ₱62,838,722.50 in compliance with Paragraph 80, IPSAS 1 for the accurate presentation of said account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books by capitalizing the borrowing costs incurred in the construction of the qualifying assets in adherence to the requirement of IPSAS              No. 5.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor, R.A.G. CORONA CONSTRUCTION AND AGGREGATES to pay for the Liquidated Damages in the amount of ₱491,032.50 equivalent to 398 days delay in the completion of the project considering that the work suspensions/time extensions requested were not supported with valid reasons.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Contractor to immediately rectify or correct the above-mentioned defects.  Otherwise, direct the contractor to pay for the cost of good quality door knobs and the cost of construction defects;
Require the Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations governing the procurement and implementation of infrastructure projects in accordance with the requirements of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer and the Committee on Inspection to conduct actual inspection on the project completion and not merely rely on the Accomplishment Report of the contractor so that deficiencies observed could be corrected by the contractor before claiming the final billing pursuant to Item 7 of Annex “E” of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Ensure that all the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November, 2020 to attain the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality;
Direct the Municipal Engineer/Project In-charge to expedite the preparation of Program of Works, Plans and Designs and other documents for all unimplemented projects under the 20% DF current and continuing appropriations so that the desired benefits and services from these projects are delivered to the target beneficiaries without delay; and
Direct the MPDC to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of all projects funded by the 20% DF so that the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a)  Direct the MDRRM Officer to prepare LDRRM Plan and to implement the planned programs, projects and activities contained in the plan based on timelines so that all targeted outputs are accomplished/completed;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unspent balance of LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2021 to the Trust Fund books pursuant COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
c)  Require the MDRRM Council to prioritize essential projects to be included in the LDRRM Plan to avoid realignment/reprogramming of projects except in time of calamities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team within five working days from execution of the contract, a copy of said contract and its supporting documents duly reviewed by his Office and approved by the Municipal Mayor as well as the basis for determining the ABC of the project to facilitate prompt technical review and evaluation by the COA, Technical Audit Specialist (TAS).

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ascertain that the signboard posted at the project site contain all the necessary information pursuant to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote full disclosure of the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Audit Team the MOA signed by the Municipal Mayor and the recipient farmer beneficiaries for every farm machinery and equipment totaling ₱2,496,000.00 as evidence that distribution was undertaken;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to check that documents attached to the claim of supplier are complete before certifying the disbursement.  Further, require the supplier to pay Liquidated Damages for delayed delivery of the equipment equivalent to one-tenth (1/10) of one (1%) of the total amount of ₱2,496,000.00 for 10 days or ₱24,960.00, pursuant to Item 3 of Annex “D” of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and  

Require the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team Pages 2 and 3 of the Invitation to Bidders as well as Pages 2 and 3 of the Purchase Order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to certify claims when supported with complete documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445;

Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to immediately submit to the Audit Team, a List of Beneficiaries with corresponding signatures for projects funded by 20% MDF:

•	Organic Farm Inputs & Other Agri Related Commodities of ₱495,450.00
•	Farm Inputs & Other Agricultural Production Support of ₱297,985.00
  
Require the BAC Secretariat to completely fill up data in the Purchase Order, particularly the Date of Delivery or Period of Delivery to facilitate computation of Liquidated Damages in case the supplier fails to deliver items within the contract period.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require the suppliers to submit an Omnibus Sworn Statement pursuant to the requirement of        RA No. 9184 and its RIRR and the GPPB.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the APP, PMRs and the APCPI in consonance with GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020 in order to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement activities.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare and submit a one-time report for all the disaster relief aids and donations to the NDRRMC, thru the OCD with a copy furnished to the respective COA Auditors, within 10 working days after the end of the quarantine or within 10 working days after the end of each quarter if quarantine exceeds three months in compliance with the requirements of Items II.1.a.2 and II.1.b.1of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a.	Instruct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to exert effort in retrieving documents pertaining to the receipt, distribution/dispersal, sale, death, etc. if any or the whereabouts and condition of Breeding Stocks; submit Status Report/Report of Physical Inventory of the Breeding Stocks as of         December 31, 2020 and submit the pertinent documents relating to their distribution/dispersal, sale, death, etc. to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for proper recording thereof;
b.	The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the Breeding Stocks. In case there are reported deaths or losses and if evidence warrant, direct the accountable personnel to request for relief from accountability for the loss of biological assets; and

c.	The Municipal Accountant derecognize the breeding stocks in the books of account, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a.	Instruct the DRRMC to formulate a comprehensive LDRRM Plan that encompasses the four thematic areas such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery pursuant to Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;  

b.	Direct the LDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in accordance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and

c.	Direct the Municipal DRRM Council to prepare a revised LDRRMF Plan by incorporating new expenditure items and COVID-related PPAs to support the release and utilization of the unexpended prior years LDRRMF, subject to the approval of the SB and the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a.	Direct the Municipal Engineer to expedite the preparation of Program of Works, Plans and Designs and other documents for all unimplemented projects under the 20% DF so that the desired benefits and services from these projects are delivered to the target beneficiaries without delay; 

b.	Ensure that all planned projects under the 20% DF are optimally utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality; and

c.	Direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of all projects funded by the 20% DF so that the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Officer and all other implementing offices to implement all projects funded by the LDRRMF based on the target timelines indicated in the MDRRM Plan to ensure optimum utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay and to equip the municipality against the adverse impacts of disaster or calamity;

Instruct the MDRRM Council to prepare a revised MDRRMF Annual Investment Plan to serve as basis in the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF transferred to the STF and ensure that the programs, projects and activities indicated in said plan be implemented based on timelines; and

Direct the MDRRM Officer to properly utilize the LDRRMF in adherence to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Expedite the procurement of additional unit garbage truck to augment the one unit currently in use to facilitate collection of residual solid wastes from the barangays;

Implement source reduction, recycling and composting programs through waste diversion activities pursuant to Section 20 of RA No. 9003 to reduce the volume of solid wastes; and 

Secure Contract of Agreement with the City Government of Cadiz to dispose its residual wastes at the City’s SLF if construction of a sanitary landfill could not be implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit actual Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year based on an updated Assessment Roll pursuant to Section 212 of RA No. 7160; and 
Require the Municipal Accountant to set up in the books RPT/SET receivables based on updated Assessment Roll pursuant to the requirements of Section 19.b, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, for fair presentation of the receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to discharge his duties with utmost diligence by posting collections and deposits in the cash book daily and reconcile his cash hand with the cash book balance pursuant to Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I and Section 111 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Manapla Municipal Government Employees Union (MMGEU) to return the amount transferred by the municipality to the union since the financial assistance violates Section 305(b) of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code. 

It is further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or designated GSO and the Municipal Officer exert effort to locate all land titles of land owned by the Municipality and process the titling of land acquired through donation, or other modes of acquisition Land Registration Authority, where necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 so that the Audit Team could conduct timely auditorial review.

We reiterated the following recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Inventory Committee to annually conduct a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s PPE as of year-end and submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year pursuant to Section 124, MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards for PPE in compliance with Section 119 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the required reconciliation of the ledger cards and property cards on PPE pursuant to Section 114, MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.
Reiterated in this report since as of year-end, the long overdue cash advances   of ₱3,354,296.42 still remained unsettled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit actual Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year based on an updated Assessment Roll pursuant to Section 212 of RA No. 7160; and 
Require the Municipal Accountant to set up in the books RPT/SET receivables based on updated Assessment Roll pursuant to the requirements of Section 19.b, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, for fair presentation of the receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to discharge his duties with utmost diligence by posting collections and deposits in the cash book daily and reconcile his cash hand with the cash book balance pursuant to Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I and Section 111 of PD 1445.

In addition, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least quarterly to come up with a reliable and accurate information of cash account as presented in the financial statement.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of   RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 by allocating 1% of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 so that the Audit Team could conduct timely auditorial review.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor –
Enforce the mandatory segregation of solid waste at source pursuant to Section 21 and 22 of RA 9003;
Direct all Barangay Chairman for the mandatory establishment of MRF in  their respective barangay in consonance with Section 32 of RA 9003; and
Call the attention of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to review annually the “Ten-Year MSWM Plan” of the Municipality pursuant to the requirement of the OIC Executive Director, NSWMC Secretariat, in his letter dated February 27, 2015.
We recommended also that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Action Officer to –
Prepare an Inventory Report for all the Equipment procured by the Municipality for the use of Solid Waste Management Office, copy furnish the Auditor, indicating the person accountable of such equipment and determine whether it is serviceable or unserviceable as of date; and
Initiate the immediate repair of dump truck as well as those equipment which can still be repaired.

It is recommended that the LSB refrain from charging non-related expenses to the Special Fund and should comply strictly with Section 272 of RA 7160 on the prioritization of expenses charged to the LSB Fund.

It is recommended that the MSWDO widen the scope of programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget which should give more focus on the welfare instead of giving preference to programs and activities to social services and livelihood.

Also, to incorporate in the GAD Plan seminars and symposia wherein the weaker members of society such as women and children be trained on how to defend themselves from bad elements of society.

It should also include in the GAD Plan programs for the welfare of out-of-school street children so they not become social menace but will have a great part in nation-building.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all dormant Advances to Officers and Employees accounts shown in the financial statements in order to ascertain the existence, accuracy and valuation, rights and obligations, and presentation that the management asserts. There is a need for management to immediately institute specific courses of action in the collection of said accounts, continue efforts in demanding the immediate liquidation of cash advances of AO who are currently employed with the Municipal Government, and implement measures to recover amounts from those who are separated from the service or have transferred to other agencies. In the event that the dormant Receivable accounts are found to be inexistent, we also recommend that the said accounts be elevated to the Commission on Audit for approval of dropping them from the books.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the daily collections intact to the AGDB as provided by Section of MNGAS for LGUs Volume I and Section 69 (4) of PD 1445, and avoid having huge amounts of undeposited collections in Cash in Vault. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the daily reporting, balancing, ruling and closing of collections in cashbooks in order to avoid shortage and overage in deposits. Moreover, as stated in Section 127 of PD 1445, in the event that the Municipal Treasurer fails to comply with the requirement imposed without any valid justification, he is considered to have constituted neglect of duty, which is a ground for administrative disciplinary action against him.

The GSO to maintain Property Cards for all properties purchased by the Municipality which should be regularly updated.  Additionally, the PAR must be renewed every three years or every time there is a change in custodianship/user of the property; and
The Accounting Office to maintain PPE ledgers cards for each asset category and Real Property cards for land owned by the Municipality.  All concerned must ensure that the documents are regularly updated to facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of LGU properties to support the Municipality’s million-peso property items as reflected in the year-end Statement of Financial Position.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a special task force to closely coordinate with the MAO will work on the titling of all real properties purchased, donated or acquired by the municipal government which has remained untitled.
It is further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Legal Officer exert effort to locate all land titles of land owned by the Municipality and process the titling of land acquired through donation, or other modes of acquisition Land Registration Authority, where necessary.

1. It is recommended that in CY 2016, the Municipal Mayor immediately create a Committee to conduct physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE, invite the Auditor or her authorized representative to witness the same, prepare the RPCPPE, and submit the report to the Auditor. 

     In order to facilitate the conduct of physical inventory, the Mayor should direct all offices and departments to conduct physical count of PPE assigned in their respective areas, and require them to submit reports on the results thereof.  Any discrepancies found between the physical count and the balance recorded in the financial statements must immediately be investigated.  Also, the Municipal Government must carry out measures to safeguard and protect government properties from losses, misuse or deterioration.

Moreover, unserviceable properties identified during the physical inventory should be summarized in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and shall be disposed of in accordance with existing regulations, whereas, personnel entrusted with the custody and control of missing properties shall immediately be determined and shall be held liable for the loss.
